course_description:
This course is designed to provide final year students a unique platform to apply and extend their knowledge across various disciplines within Business, Commerce and Tourism. Students will collaborate closely with a diverse team of peers to devise innovative solutions and strategies for novel, complex challenges presented by industry partners. Students will gain project management skills, learn to practically apply academic learnings in a multidisciplinary team context, and develop a comprehensive, workplace-ready skill set to transition smoothly into the professional world. Information about how to apply for RBUS7888 can be found on the BEL Faculty's work-integrated learning website .

Students are allocated to a small project team that will provide a business solution to an industry partner. Project work occur continuously throughout the semester and is supervised by a facilitator/tutor. Students are required to do in-person and/or virtual site visits to the main operations of the industry partner to gain contextual knowledge of the project challenge and to conduct research for the successful completion of the project. The project culminates in an oral presentation and written report to the industry partner.

For more information on applying to be accepted to this course please consult https://bel.uq.edu.au/for-students/careers/wil .

Course Changes in Response to Previous Student Feedback

The end-of-semester applied professional practice exam has been discontinued because it could not adequately reflect students’ real project experiences. As a result, the weighting of the personal critical reflection assessment has been increased to 30%.

prerequisites: You'll need to complete the following courses before enrolling in this one:
GPA of 4.0 or higher. Approval required from Head of School via Business School Postgraduate Student Liaison Team.
restrictions: Restricted to students in the final year of the MBus, MCom, MEI and MTHEM programs.

timetable:
The timetable for this course is available on the
UQ Public Timetable
.
Additional timetable information
Each project will be allocated a facilitator/tutor who will meet with the project team(s) once a week. This facilitator/tutor meeting time will be negotiated with the tutor and team members, and attendance is required.
In addition to meeting with their tutors, teams will collaborate continuously and independently throughout the semester to ensure all project objectives are met.
You must check the course's
learn.uq
site as well as your university email address for course announcements, updates and additional information throughout the semester.
Should you need help with your timetable and/or allocation of classes, email
business.mytimetable@uq.edu.au
from your UQ student email account.
course_aims: This course aims to provide students with the opportunity to apply conceptual knowledge to the real world via an industry project. As such, this course links discipline learnings, skills, and attributes developed by students across the duration of their studies with the challenges and expectations of working with an industry partner on a consultancy project to solve complex business problems. In doing so, students have the opportunity to gain valuable consultancy, project-based, and professional work skills, alongside advanced managerial capabilities to navigate complex project challenges. This experience allows students to reflect on the lessons learned and consider how these can be applied to enhance their future professional practice and support long-term career development.

learning_outcomes.description: Apply interdisciplinary knowledge to critically analyse and solve complex real-world industry problems in an innovative manner.
Demonstrate professional practice, project management, consultancy, and group work skills through collaborative problem solving.
Persuasively pitch and defend business solutions and recommendations in relation to the industry partner's problem.
Present a business report that reflects continuous feedback and delivers a viable solution to the industry problem.
Critically evaluate your strengths and areas for growth, considering team interactions and project execution.
Apply lessons from the project experience for future professional practice and career plans.
Demonstrate a managerial mindset by applying critical thinking, strategic decision-making, and leadership to effectively analyse and address complex project challenges.

assessment_details.title: A1: Project preparation
assessment_details.due_date: A1.1 Team setup 10/03/2026 2:00 pm A1.2 Preliminary project report 17/03/2026 2:00 pm
assessment_details.other_conditions: Work integrated learning, Secure. See the conditions definitions
assessment_details.task_description: In the opening stage of the project your project team will demonstrate foundational planning for the execution of the project. You and your team will prepare and present your team setup arrangements (communication, collaboration/meetings, rules, conflict resolution strategies, reflection, quality control, etc.), as well as a preliminary project plan (scope of work, work breakdown structure, team member roles and responsibilities, project timeline, milestones, risk contingency plan, etc.). This task reflects standard project initiation practices in industry, where teams establish ways of working and develop formal project plans that are essential for effective project delivery. Assessment criteria will cover team setup decisions and the application of project planning principles and methods. AI Statement: Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
Deferral or extension
You may be able to apply for an extension .
The maximum extension allowed is 7 days. Extensions are given in multiples of 24 hours.
assessment_details.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension .

assessment_details.title: A2: Critical reflection
assessment_details.other_conditions: Student specific, Work integrated learning. See the conditions definitions
assessment_details.task_description: In this assessment, you will critically reflect on your project’s progression and execution, highlighting key incidents/moments/challenges/successes. You will draw lessons from your and your team’s planning and management of the project. You will also evaluate your individual contributions, the skills and attributes you demonstrated, and areas for personal development. This task will help you build reflective habits that support effective teamwork and accountability. It mirrors common workplace practices such as project debriefs, lessons-learned reviews, and professional development planning. Assessment criteria will assess students’ ability to identify and describe relevant critical incidents or moments, critically analyse those incidents or moments, and articulate the personal and professional lessons derived. AI Statement: Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
Deferral or extension
You may be able to apply for an extension .
The maximum extension allowed is 7 days. Extensions are given in multiples of 24 hours.
Late submission
A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.

assessment_details.title: A3: Project deliverables
assessment_details.due_date: A3.1 Client presentation 18/05/2026 - 22/05/2026 A3.2 Final report 25/05/2026 - 29/05/2026 The exact day and time of the client presentation will be determined in negotiation with the industry partner. Failure to present to the industry partner on the agreed date and time will be indicative of gross professional failure and will result in a mark of zero.
assessment_details.other_conditions: Peer assessed, Peer assessment factor, Work integrated learning. See the conditions definitions
assessment_details.task_description: The project culminates in the delivery of a client presentation and final report containing your project team’s research, analysis and recommendations. A3.1: The client presentation will be a distilled version of the report, delivered via PowerPoint, either in-person or through video conferencing, depending on the client’s preferences. You will take responsibility for and present part of the presentation, on which you will be individually assessed. Assessment criteria will cover organisation and clarity of information, use of audio-visual aids, oral delivery, and strength of arguments. A3.2: The final report will be an industry standard document containing a complete account of the work of the project team, including methodology, data collected, analysis and findings/recommendations. Assessment criteria will cover report structure, writing quality, analysis and findings, and use of visual aids. This is a group task, and a peer assessment factor will be applied to the grade for the final report. AI Statement: Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
The slide deck and report should NOT be handed to the industry partner unless reviewed and approved by the tutor.
Deferral or extension
You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
Late submission
Extensions or deferrals are not available for this presentation due to industry partner timetables.

assessment_details.title: A4: PM tools and practices
assessment_details.due_date: 29/05/2026 5:00 pm The version of the MS Teams workspace as it exists on the assessment due date will be used for marking. No blackboard submission required.
assessment_details.other_conditions: Peer assessed, Peer assessment factor, Work integrated learning. See the conditions definitions
assessment_details.task_description: This assessment is designed to develop and test students’ use of key project management tools and practices in an authentic industry context. You and your team will use MS Teams as the central platform for collaboration and coordination throughout the project. Upon completion of the project, your team will be evaluated on the effective use of the MSTeams ecosystem, your team’s management of working documents, and your meeting management practices. This is a group task, and a peer assessment factor will be applied to the grade for this task. Assessment criteria will cover use of the MSTeams discussion board, document protocols, and meeting management practices. Read full task description Hide full task description

Deferral or extension
You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.

learning_resources.library_resources: Library resources are available on the UQ Library website .

learning_activities.topic: Meet your team Students meet the Course Coordinator, WIL staff, facilitators/tutors and are assembled in their respective teams. This is an important coordination session as teams need to negotiate communication protocols and meeting times for the rest of the semester. Learning outcomes: L02

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeks From Week 2 To Week 13
learning_activities.topic: Tutorials Students will attend tutorials once a week during the course of the semester. The tutorials are meant to help students plan and manage their project, track progress, address issues/problems, and receive general guidance from their tutor. The meeting times and locations for the tutorials are negotiated between the students and their tutors at the 'Meet your team' lecture in Week 1. Learning outcomes: L01, L02

Course introduction and group work essentials The seminar will include: Course introduction Course Profile Assessment requirements Group work essentials Collaboration tools and techniques Types of teams Characteristics of effective teams Stages of team development Effective communication in a team Conflict resolution strategies Professionalism and expectations Learning outcomes: L02

Project planning essentials In this seminar we will explore: Project planning essentials Thinking differently about solving problems How to interpret the project brief and client needs Project scoping and feasibility Defining project objectives and deliverables Managing the client relationship Expectation management Types and frequency of communication Learning outcomes: L02
Project preparation presentations Learning outcomes: L02

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeks From Week 5 To Week 9
learning_activities.topic: Project progress workshops Weeks 5 -9 will be devoted to intensive in-person project progress consultations with the lecturer. Learning outcomes: L01, L02

Presentation skills and report writing The seminar will include: Understanding the purpose and audience Structure of a presentation Time management Visual aids Oral delivery Engagement techniques Discussion/Q&A Learning outcomes: L03, L04
Rehearsals Teams present their findings to teaching staff for feedback. Learning outcomes: L03
Client presentations Teams present their solution to their business partner. Learning outcomes: L01, L03
Course wrap-up Discussion of learnings from the client presentations. Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05, L06, L07
